title: 3. “The Thumbs” | Gene and Roger
format: Brian Raftery's Siskel-and-Ebert history reaches 1982: the SNL cameo, the jump from PBS's Sneak Previews to syndicated At the Movies, and the debut of thumbs up / thumbs down.

segments.heading: From Sneak Previews to the thumbs

films.title: Swamp Thing
films.note: Used to show how the critics championed movies their colleagues dismissed. A monster-movie review on PBS, with Ebert calling the comic-book flick terrific.

films.title: Apocalypse Now
films.note: A clip of the two arguing it out, praising the scale and the Sheen and Duvall performances; Gene later changed his mind, and they eventually devoted a whole special to Vietnam movies.

films.title: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
films.note: The film big enough that Justin Lin's father closed the family fish-and-chips shop early to take the kids to a 10 p.m. show at Cerritos Mall.

films.title: Mr. Mom
films.note: Lin remembers Siskel and Ebert reviewing it and wondering aloud what it would be like with the guy from Splash, before Tom Hanks was Tom Hanks.

films.title: Splash
films.note: Invoked in that same review as the Tom Hanks calling card, the moment Lin first grasped that movies get cast.

films.title: 99 Homes
films.note: One of Ramin Bahrani's films; as a kid in Winston-Salem he learned about independent and foreign movies from the show.

films.title: The White Tiger
films.note: Bahrani's later film, cited alongside 99 Homes to place the director whose film education ran through Siskel and Ebert.

films.title: Better Luck Tomorrow
films.note: Justin Lin's breakout, the first film named in his path from watching the critics to directing.

films.title: Fast Five
films.note: Lin's Fast entry, singled out as the one where they drag the safe through Rio.

films.title: F9
films.note: Lin's then-current Fast film, released the same summer this episode aired.

films.title: Strange Brew
films.note: The comedy that asked Gene and Roger to play themselves; they declined.

films.title: Terms of Endearment
films.note: Two thumbs up on At the Movies, the segment used to introduce the new thumbs-up / thumbs-down verdict.

films.title: Twilight Zone: The Movie
films.note: A close split in the same reel: a marginal thumbs up from Gene, a close thumbs down from Roger.

films.title: Psycho II
films.note: Two downturned thumbs, dismissed as a well-made but weak carbon copy of the Hitchcock original.

films.title: Three Amigos
films.note: Roger panned it on Carson with Chevy Chase sitting right next to him, proof of how much weight the thumbs now carried.

referenced.group: The era's vital mainstream movies (named in passing)
films: Richard Pryor: Live on the Sunset Strip

TV: The Tonight Show with Johnny Carson

Richard Pryor: Live on the Sunset Strip.title: Richard Pryor: Live on the Sunset Strip
